pattern: Rotating a square matrix by 90 degrees four times returns it to the start, so just compare the target against mat after 0, 1, 2, and 3 rotations.
hints: There are only four distinct rotations of a square matrix, so you can enumerate all of them.
A 90-degree clockwise rotation maps the transpose with reversed rows; apply it repeatedly.
Return true if any of the four rotated versions equals the target exactly.

edge_inputs.description: no rotation can match different bit counts
1x1 with different single value cannot match

stress.description: random binary square plus a guaranteed-rotatable target via random rotation count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    mat = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    t = [row[:] for row in mat]
    for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 3)):
        t = [list(row) for row in zip(*t[::-1])]
    return [mat, t]

stress.description: two independent random binary squares, usually not rotations of each other
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    mat = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    target = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [mat, target]
